summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the service with prayer, asking God for a customized message for each person. He introduced the sermon's theme: the three components of real, lasting life change. The first component is that true change begins with a genuine encounter with Jesus Christ. Pastor Travis emphasized that many people start their faith journey with this encounter but stop there, missing the fullness of God's plan. He illustrated this point with the dramatic conversion of Saul (Paul) in Acts 9, highlighting how God actively pursues us, even in our rebellion, and meets us in our darkest moments. The second component is that after an encounter with Christ, you will never be the same again. Using the story of the lame man healed by Peter and John in Acts 3, Pastor Travis explained that God's power brings instant transformation. He encouraged the congregation to stop holding themselves and others hostage to past identities, as God is continually at work making people new. The third component is sanctification, which Pastor Travis defined as God's continual process of making us more holy and more like Christ. He explained that this is an ongoing journey of growth, supported by the Holy Spirit dwelling within us and by discipleship within Christian community. The message concluded with an invitation to surrender to Christ, a call for baptism, and a reminder to publicly celebrate and share the life-changing work God is doing.
mainPoints: Real life change begins with a true, personal encounter with Jesus Christ.
An encounter with Christ brings transformation; you are made new and will never be the same.
Sanctification is God's continual process of making us more holy and more like Him.

versesMentioned.text: Meanwhile, Saul was still breathing out murderous threats against the Lord’s disciples. He went to the high priest and asked him for letters to the synagogues in Damascus, so that if he found any there who belonged to the Way, whether men or women, he might take them as prisoners to Jerusalem. As he neared Damascus on his journey, suddenly a light from heaven flashed around him. He fell to the ground and heard a voice say to him, 'Saul, Saul, why do you persecute me?' 'Who are you, Lord?' Saul asked. 'I am Jesus, whom you are persecuting,' he replied. 'Now get up and go into the city, and you will be told what you must do.' The men traveling with Saul stood there speechless; they heard the sound but did not see anyone. Saul got up from the ground, but when he opened his eyes he could see nothing. So they led him by the hand into Damascus. For three days he was blind, and did not eat or drink.'

Then Jesus told them this parable: 'Suppose one of you has a hundred sheep and loses one of them. Doesn’t he leave the ninety-nine in the open country and go after the lost sheep until he finds it? And when he finds it, he joyfully puts it on his shoulders and goes home. Then he calls his friends and neighbors together and says, ‘Rejoice with me; I have found my lost sheep.’ I tell you that in the same way there will be more rejoicing in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous persons who do not need to repent.'
Immediately, something like scales fell from Saul’s eyes, and he could see again. He got up and was baptized.
Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come: The old has gone, the new is here!

One day Peter and John were going up to the temple at the time of prayer—at three in the afternoon. Now a man who was lame from birth was being carried to the temple gate called Beautiful, where he was put every day to beg from those going into the temple courts. When he saw Peter and John about to enter, he asked them for money. Peter looked straight at him, as did John. Then Peter said, 'Look at us!' So the man gave them his attention, expecting to get something from them. Then Peter said, 'Silver or gold I do not have, but what I do have I give you. In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, walk.' Taking him by the right hand, he helped him up, and instantly the man’s feet and ankles became strong. He jumped to his feet and began to walk. Then he went with them into the temple courts, walking and jumping, and praising God. When all the people saw him walking and praising God, they recognized him as the same man who used to sit begging at the temple gate called Beautiful, and they were filled with wonder and amazement at what had happened to him.
For I am not ashamed of the gospel, because it is the power of God that brings salvation to everyone who believes: first to the Jew, then to the Gentile.
Whoever is ashamed of me and my words, the Son of Man will be ashamed of them when he comes in his glory and in the glory of the Father and of the holy angels.

You are the light of the world. A town built on a hill cannot be hidden. Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a bowl. Instead they put it on its stand, and it gives light to everyone in the house. In the same way, let your light shine before others, that they may see your good deeds and glorify your Father in heaven.
Don’t you know that you yourselves are God’s temple and that God’s Spirit dwells in your midst?
May God himself, the God of peace, sanctify you through and through. May your whole spirit, soul and body be kept blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
He must become greater; I must become less.

Monday.scripture: Acts 9:1-9 - '...suddenly a light from heaven flashed around him. He fell to the ground and heard a voice...'
Monday.action: Reflect on a time you felt God was pursuing you, even if you didn't recognize it at the moment.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you for never giving up on me. Open my eyes to see how you are actively chasing me with your love today.

Tuesday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 5:17 - 'Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come...'
Tuesday.action: Write down one area where you are not the same person you used to be because of Christ.
Tuesday.prayer: God, I celebrate that I am a new creation in you. Help me to live today in the freedom of my new identity.

Wednesday.scripture: Acts 3:8 - 'He jumped to his feet and began to walk. Then he went with them into the temple courts, walking and jumping, and praising God.'
Wednesday.action: Share one thing God has done for you this week with a friend or family member.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, I choose not to hide your goodness. Give me courage to let my light shine and praise you publicly.

Thursday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 3:16 - 'Don’t you know that you yourselves are God’s temple and that God’s Spirit dwells in your midst?'
Thursday.action: Take a moment of quiet to acknowledge God's Spirit living within you.
Thursday.prayer: Holy Spirit, thank you for making your home in me. Guide my thoughts, words, and actions to be more like Christ today.

Friday.scripture: 1 Thessalonians 5:23 - 'May God himself, the God of peace, sanctify you through and through...'
Friday.action: Identify one habit or thought pattern you are trusting God to change, and be patient with yourself in the process.
Friday.prayer: God of peace, I surrender to your sanctifying work. Make me more like Jesus, one step at a time.

Saturday.scripture: John 3:30 - 'He must become greater; I must become less.'
Saturday.action: Intentionally choose to put someone else's needs before your own today.
Saturday.prayer: Jesus, I want more of you and less of me. In my decisions and interactions today, let your love and character increase in my life.
